content.content:
1	# Production override for running the stack on a server reachable off localhost.
2	#
3	# It closes the local-dev conveniences the base compose leaves open: Postgres is no longer published
4	# to the host network (the app still reaches it over the compose network), and the app binds to
5	# localhost so a reverse proxy terminating TLS is the only public entrypoint. Layer it on the base:
6	#
7	#   APP_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always) \
8	#     docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml --profile full up -d --build
9	#
10	# Put your secrets (INGEST_TOKEN, INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true, GEMINI_API_KEY) in a .env next to these
11	# files — see "Securing an exposed deployment" and "Remote deployment" in the README.
12	#
13	# Requires Docker Compose v2.24+ (Jan 2024) for the `!reset` / `!override` merge tags below — on an
14	# older Compose they're ignored and the base ports would leak through, defeating the point.
15	services:
16	  postgres:
17	    # Don't expose the database to the network; only the app needs it, over the compose network.
18	    # `!reset` replaces the base list (a plain `ports: []` would be *appended*, leaving 5432 published).
19	    ports: !reset []
20	
21	  app:
22	    # `!override` replaces the base list rather than appending to it, so 8080 isn't also left published
23	    # on all interfaces. Bind to localhost only — a reverse proxy (nginx / Caddy / a cloud LB) fronts
24	    # it and terminates TLS.
25	    ports: !override
26	      - "127.0.0.1:8080:8080"
27

file.content:
# Production override for running the stack on a server reachable off localhost.
#
# It closes the local-dev conveniences the base compose leaves open: Postgres is no longer published
# to the host network (the app still reaches it over the compose network), and the app binds to
# localhost so a reverse proxy terminating TLS is the only public entrypoint. Layer it on the base:
#
#   APP_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always) \
#     docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml --profile full up -d --build
#
# Put your secrets (INGEST_TOKEN, INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true, GEMINI_API_KEY) in a .env next to these
# files — see "Securing an exposed deployment" and "Remote deployment" in the README.
#
# Requires Docker Compose v2.24+ (Jan 2024) for the `!reset` / `!override` merge tags below — on an
# older Compose they're ignored and the base ports would leak through, defeating the point.
services:
  postgres:
    # Don't expose the database to the network; only the app needs it, over the compose network.
    # `!reset` replaces the base list (a plain `ports: []` would be *appended*, leaving 5432 published).
    ports: !reset []

  app:
    # `!override` replaces the base list rather than appending to it, so 8080 isn't also left published
    # on all interfaces. Bind to localhost only — a reverse proxy (nginx / Caddy / a cloud LB) fronts
    # it and terminates TLS.
    ports: !override
      - "127.0.0.1:8080:8080"

content.content:
1	# Agent Brain Visualizer — sample configuration
2	#
3	# Copy this file to `.env` and edit it:
4	#
5	#     cp .env.example .env
6	#
7	# `.env` is gitignored, so your API key never gets committed.
8	#
9	# Precedence: real environment variables > .env > built-in defaults.
10	# Run with `-Ddotenv.enabled=false` to ignore the file entirely, or
11	# `-Ddotenv.path=/some/other/.env` to load it from elsewhere.
12	#
13	# Format notes: `KEY=value` per line. Full-line `#` comments and blank lines are
14	# ignored. Quote a value ("like this") to keep spaces or a literal `#`.
15	
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# AI provider — `gemini` (default, hosted) or `ollama` (local, no key needed)
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	AI_PROVIDER=gemini
20	
21	# --- Option A: Google Gemini (required when AI_PROVIDER=gemini) -------------
22	# Get a key at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
23	GEMINI_API_KEY=your-api-key-here
24	
25	# Optional. Defaults to gemini-3.5-flash
26	# GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash
27	
28	# --- Option B: local model via Ollama ---------------------------------------
29	# Set AI_PROVIDER=ollama above, run `ollama serve`, and pull a model first.
30	# No GEMINI_API_KEY is needed in this mode.
31	# OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
32	# OLLAMA_MODEL=gemma4
33	
34	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	# Server
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	# Optional. Defaults to 8080
38	# MICRONAUT_SERVER_PORT=8080
39	
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	# Trajectory store (Postgres)
42	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	# Sessions, cached AI analyses, and eval runs live here rather than on local
44	# disk, so trajectories captured on one machine are visible from another.
45	#
46	# For local development, start the checked-in container — its credentials are
47	# exactly the defaults below, so you can leave this whole section commented out:
48	#
49	#     docker compose up -d
50	#
51	# To share trajectories across machines, point every machine at one hosted
52	# Postgres (Neon, Supabase, Cloud SQL, ...) instead.
53	# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/agentbrainviz
54	# POSTGRES_USER=agentviz
55	# POSTGRES_PASSWORD=REDACTED
56	
57	# Hosted providers usually require TLS; append it to the URL, e.g.
58	# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/db?sslmode=require
59	
60	# The /api/ingest endpoints are the only ones that write, and the only ones a
61	# remote machine calls. Once the server is reachable off localhost, set a shared
62	# secret here and pass the same value to the ingest client. Unset leaves them open
63	# (fine on localhost); the server warns at startup whenever ingest is unauthenticated.
64	# INGEST_TOKEN=
65	
66	# Fail closed: when true, ingest is refused until INGEST_TOKEN is set, so a missing
67	# token can't silently leave the write endpoints exposed. Leave unset for the open
68	# localhost default.
69	# INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true
70	
71	# Log format (JVM / Docker; the native binary logs text). Default is CONSOLE_TEXT
72	# (human-readable, colorized). Set exactly CONSOLE_JSON for structured logs — one JSON
73	# object per line — for a log aggregator. A typo leaves the logger with no appender.
74	# LOG_APPENDER=CONSOLE_JSON
75	
76

file.content:
# Agent Brain Visualizer — sample configuration
#
# Copy this file to `.env` and edit it:
#
#     cp .env.example .env
#
# `.env` is gitignored, so your API key never gets committed.
#
# Precedence: real environment variables > .env > built-in defaults.
# Run with `-Ddotenv.enabled=false` to ignore the file entirely, or
# `-Ddotenv.path=/some/other/.env` to load it from elsewhere.
#
# Format notes: `KEY=value` per line. Full-line `#` comments and blank lines are
# ignored. Quote a value ("like this") to keep spaces or a literal `#`.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AI provider — `gemini` (default, hosted) or `ollama` (local, no key needed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AI_PROVIDER=gemini

# --- Option A: Google Gemini (required when AI_PROVIDER=gemini) -------------
# Get a key at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
GEMINI_API_KEY=your-api-key-here

# Optional. Defaults to gemini-3.5-flash
# GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash

# --- Option B: local model via Ollama ---------------------------------------
# Set AI_PROVIDER=ollama above, run `ollama serve`, and pull a model first.
# No GEMINI_API_KEY is needed in this mode.
# OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
# OLLAMA_MODEL=gemma4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Server
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional. Defaults to 8080
# MICRONAUT_SERVER_PORT=8080

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Trajectory store (Postgres)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sessions, cached AI analyses, and eval runs live here rather than on local
# disk, so trajectories captured on one machine are visible from another.
#
# For local development, start the checked-in container — its credentials are
# exactly the defaults below, so you can leave this whole section commented out:
#
#     docker compose up -d
#
# To share trajectories across machines, point every machine at one hosted
# Postgres (Neon, Supabase, Cloud SQL, ...) instead.
# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/agentbrainviz
# POSTGRES_USER=agentviz
# POSTGRES_PASSWORD=REDACTED

# Hosted providers usually require TLS; append it to the URL, e.g.
# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/db?sslmode=require

# The /api/ingest endpoints are the only ones that write, and the only ones a
# remote machine calls. Once the server is reachable off localhost, set a shared
# secret here and pass the same value to the ingest client. Unset leaves them open
# (fine on localhost); the server warns at startup whenever ingest is unauthenticated.
# INGEST_TOKEN=

# Fail closed: when true, ingest is refused until INGEST_TOKEN is set, so a missing
# token can't silently leave the write endpoints exposed. Leave unset for the open
# localhost default.
# INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true

# Log format (JVM / Docker; the native binary logs text). Default is CONSOLE_TEXT
# (human-readable, colorized). Set exactly CONSOLE_JSON for structured logs — one JSON
# object per line — for a log aggregator. A typo leaves the logger with no appender.
# LOG_APPENDER=CONSOLE_JSON

      # requires `docker compose down -v` (wipes data) or an in-database ALTER ROLE.
      POSTGRES_DB: agentbrainviz

# Docker Compose for the Agent Brain Visualizer.
#
#   Database only (for local development / tests — the default):
#     docker compose up -d                       # start Postgres; data persists in the pgdata volume
#     docker compose up -d postgres              # same, explicit
#
#   Whole stack (app + database) in Docker:
#     docker compose --profile full up -d --build   # build the app image and run it against Postgres
#
#   Stop:
#     docker compose down        # stop, keeping the database volume
#     docker compose down -v     # stop and WIPE the database
#
# The credentials below match the defaults in `.env.example`, so `./gradlew run` (or the app
# container) works against this database with no extra configuration.
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:17-alpine
    container_name: agent-brain-viz-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: agentbrainviz
      POSTGRES_USER: agentviz
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: agentviz
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      # Named volume: the data survives `docker compose down`, restarts, and app-image rebuilds.
      # It is removed only by `docker compose down -v`.
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U agentviz -d agentbrainviz"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10

  app:
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        # Stamp the image version (shown at GET /health). Set to a release tag or `git describe`;
        # defaults to "docker" for an unlabelled local build.
        APP_VERSION: ${APP_VERSION:-docker}
    image: agent-brain-viz:local
    container_name: agent-brain-viz-app
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Only starts with `--profile full`, so the default `docker compose up` stays database-only for
    # local development (where you run the app with `./gradlew run` on the host).
    profiles: ["full"]
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      # Reach Postgres over the compose network by service name, not localhost.
      DATABASE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/agentbrainviz
      POSTGRES_USER: agentviz
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: agentviz
      # Optional. Passed through from the host shell or the project's .env if set; otherwise blank,
      # and AI summarization is simply unavailable.
      AI_PROVIDER: ${AI_PROVIDER:-gemini}
      GEMINI_API_KEY: ${GEMINI_API_KEY:-}
      # Set this once the app is reachable off localhost, and pass the same value to agent-ingest.
      INGEST_TOKEN: ${INGEST_TOKEN:-}
      # Fail closed: with the container running -Ddotenv.enabled=false, the app only sees env vars
      # passed here (not a .env), so this must be forwarded explicitly for the flag to take effect.
      INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH: ${INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH:-}
      # Structured JSON logs by default in the container (one object per line, for a log aggregator).
      # Override with LOG_APPENDER=CONSOLE_TEXT for human-readable container logs.
      LOG_APPENDER: ${LOG_APPENDER:-CONSOLE_JSON}
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    healthcheck:
      # No curl in the JRE image, so use bash's /dev/tcp to make a minimal HTTP request to the
      # liveness probe and confirm a 200. Liveness (not readiness) on purpose: the app serves the UI
      # even when the store is down, so a database blip must not mark the container unhealthy.
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "bash -c 'exec 3<>/dev/tcp/localhost/8080 && printf \"GET /health HTTP/1.0\\r\\n\\r\\n\" >&3 && grep -q \"200 OK\" <&3'"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

volumes:
  pgdata:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Production override for running the stack on a server reachable off localhost.
#
# It closes the local-dev conveniences the base compose leaves open: Postgres is no longer published
# to the host network (the app still reaches it over the compose network), and the app binds to
# localhost so a reverse proxy terminating TLS is the only public entrypoint. Layer it on the base:
#
#   APP_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always) \
#     docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml --profile full up -d --build
#
# Put your secrets (INGEST_TOKEN, INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true, GEMINI_API_KEY) in a .env next to these
# files — see "Securing an exposed deployment" and "Remote deployment" in the README.
#
# Requires Docker Compose v2.24+ (Jan 2024) for the `!reset` / `!override` merge tags below — on an
# older Compose they're ignored and the base ports would leak through, defeating the point.
services:
  postgres:
    # Don't expose the database to the network; only the app needs it, over the compose network.
    # `!reset` replaces the base list (a plain `ports: []` would be *appended*, leaving 5432 published).
    ports: !reset []

  app:
    # `!override` replaces the base list rather than appending to it, so 8080 isn't also left published
    # on all interfaces. Bind to localhost only — a reverse proxy (nginx / Caddy / a cloud LB) fronts
    # it and terminates TLS.
    ports: !override
      - "127.0.0.1:8080:8080"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Agent Brain Visualizer — sample configuration
#
# Copy this file to `.env` and edit it:
#
#     cp .env.example .env
#
# `.env` is gitignored, so your API key never gets committed.
#
# Precedence: real environment variables > .env > built-in defaults.
# Run with `-Ddotenv.enabled=false` to ignore the file entirely, or
# `-Ddotenv.path=/some/other/.env` to load it from elsewhere.
#
# Format notes: `KEY=value` per line. Full-line `#` comments and blank lines are
# ignored. Quote a value ("like this") to keep spaces or a literal `#`.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AI provider — `gemini` (default, hosted) or `ollama` (local, no key needed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AI_PROVIDER=gemini

# --- Option A: Google Gemini (required when AI_PROVIDER=gemini) -------------
# Get a key at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
GEMINI_API_KEY=your-api-key-here

# Optional. Defaults to gemini-3.5-flash
# GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash

# --- Option B: local model via Ollama ---------------------------------------
# Set AI_PROVIDER=ollama above, run `ollama serve`, and pull a model first.
# No GEMINI_API_KEY is needed in this mode.
# OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
# OLLAMA_MODEL=gemma4

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Server
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional. Defaults to 8080
# MICRONAUT_SERVER_PORT=8080

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Trajectory store (Postgres)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sessions, cached AI analyses, and eval runs live here rather than on local
# disk, so trajectories captured on one machine are visible from another.
#
# For local development, start the checked-in container — its credentials are
# exactly the defaults below, so you can leave this whole section commented out:
#
#     docker compose up -d
#
# To share trajectories across machines, point every machine at one hosted
# Postgres (Neon, Supabase, Cloud SQL, ...) instead.
# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/agentbrainviz
# POSTGRES_USER=agentviz
# POSTGRES_PASSWORD=REDACTED

# Hosted providers usually require TLS; append it to the URL, e.g.
# DATABASE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/db?sslmode=require

# The /api/ingest endpoints are the only ones that write, and the only ones a
# remote machine calls. Once the server is reachable off localhost, set a shared
# secret here and pass the same value to the ingest client. Unset leaves them open
# (fine on localhost); the server warns at startup whenever ingest is unauthenticated.
# INGEST_TOKEN=

# Fail closed: when true, ingest is refused until INGEST_TOKEN is set, so a missing
# token can't silently leave the write endpoints exposed. Leave unset for the open
# localhost default.
# INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true

# Log format (JVM / Docker; the native binary logs text). Default is CONSOLE_TEXT
# (human-readable, colorized). Set exactly CONSOLE_JSON for structured logs — one JSON
# object per line — for a log aggregator. A typo leaves the logger with no appender.
# LOG_APPENDER=CONSOLE_JSON

lines: +# Guards the rest of the API — the stored transcripts and the endpoints that spend
+# LLM tokens (summarize, mine, optimize, eval's judge) or mutate saved eval runs.
+# Set it alongside INGEST_TOKEN once the server is reachable off localhost; the web
+# UI prompts for it once and remembers it in the browser. Kept separate from
+# INGEST_TOKEN on purpose: the ingest token goes to every pushing machine, the API
+# token only to people allowed to read.
+# Fail closed for the API, mirroring INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH.
# Log format (JVM / Docker; the native binary logs text). Default is CONSOLE_TEXT
# (human-readable, colorized). Set exactly CONSOLE_JSON for structured logs — one JSON
# object per line — for a log aggregator. A typo leaves the logger with no appender.

content.content:
185	server does the parsing**, reusing the same adapters the UI relies on, so a client never has to
186	understand Antigravity's, Codex's, or Claude Code's file formats — and fixing an adapter fixes every
187	machine by upgrading the server alone.
188	
189	Nothing duplicates. A trajectory is keyed by `(source, id)`, where the id comes from the transcript
190	itself (a Claude Code session UUID, a Codex rollout id, an Antigravity session directory) rather than
191	the path it was found at. Pushing the same session twice, or from two machines, updates one row —
192	and re-pushing unchanged content is a no-op the server reports as `skipped`.
193	
194	These are the only endpoints that write. Set `INGEST_TOKEN` once the server is reachable from
195	another machine, and clients must then send `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
196	
197	#### Securing an exposed deployment
198	
199	Run on localhost only and the defaults are fine. The moment the server is reachable from another
200	machine — including any `docker compose --profile full up`, which publishes port 8080 on all
201	interfaces — treat it as exposed and do the following:
202	
203	- **Set `INGEST_TOKEN`.** Without it, the write endpoints are open to anyone who can reach the port,
204	  and a large pushed body can pin significant heap (the request is buffered whole). The server logs a
205	  warning at startup whenever ingest is unauthenticated, so watch the boot log. Give the same token to
206	  `agent-ingest` via `AGENT_INGEST_TOKEN`.
207	- **Fail closed if you never want the open default.** Set `INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true` and the server
208	  refuses all ingest until a token is configured, so a missing `INGEST_TOKEN` can't silently leave the
209	  writes exposed.
210	- **Terminate TLS at a reverse proxy.** The app speaks plain HTTP; put nginx, Caddy, or a cloud load
211	  balancer in front to serve HTTPS, so the bearer token and transcripts aren't sent in the clear. If
212	  the proxy runs on the same host, keep `INGEST_TOKEN` set — an unauthenticated request forwarded from
213	  a local proxy is indistinguishable from a genuine localhost client.
214	- **Don't publish Postgres.** Keep the database on a private network; only the app needs to reach it.
215	  The default compose file exposes 5432 for local development — remove that port mapping in a shared
216	  deployment.
217	
218	Read endpoints stay open (this is a single-user tool); put the whole app behind the proxy's auth if
219	you need to restrict who can view trajectories.
220	
221	> [!NOTE]
222	> The eval run history previously lived in `~/.agybrainviz/eval-runs.jsonl`. That file is no longer
223	> read, so a history saved before this change won't appear. Nothing deletes it — you can remove it
224	> by hand once you're happy.
225	
226	#### Remote / production deployment
227	
228	To run the stack on a **server** that other machines reach over the network, layer the checked-in
229	production override (`docker-compose.prod.yml`) on the base compose. It closes the local-dev
230	conveniences: Postgres is no longer published to the network, and the app binds to localhost so a
231	reverse proxy is the only public entrypoint.
232	
233	1. **Put your secrets in a `.env`** next to the compose files (compose reads it automatically), then
234	   `chmod 600 .env`:
235	
236	   ```dotenv
237	   INGEST_TOKEN=<openssl rand -hex 32>
238	   INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true         # refuse ingest until the token is set (fail closed)
239	   GEMINI_API_KEY=<optional, for AI summaries>
240	   ```
241	
242	2. **Bring up the stack**, stamping the image with a version so you can tell builds apart:
243	
244	   ```bash
245	   APP_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always) \
246	     docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml --profile full up -d --build
247	   curl -s localhost:8080/health        # {"status":"UP","version":"<your version>"}
248	   ```
249	
250	   `GET /health` reports the running build's version, so you can confirm what's deployed at a glance
251	   (the CLI reports its own with `agent-ingest --version`).
252	
253	3. **Terminate TLS at a reverse proxy** — Caddy gets you HTTPS with almost no config:
254	
255	   ```
256	   # Caddyfile
257	   viz.example.com {
258	       reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:8080
259	   }
260	   ```
261	
262	   See **Securing an exposed deployment** above for the full checklist (token, fail-closed, don't
263	   publish Postgres — the override already handles that last one).
264	
265	4. **Push from each machine that has agent transcripts** — the transcripts live on your dev machines,
266	   not the server:
267	
268	   ```bash
269	   export AGENT_INGEST_TOKEN=<the same token>
270	   agent-ingest --server https://viz.example.com
271	   ```
272	
273	   Install `agent-ingest` from a [release](cli/README.md#install), or build it from source.
274	
275	The `pgdata` volume keeps the store across restarts and rebuilds. To update: `git pull` and re-run the
276	same `up -d --build` command; the data is untouched.
277	
278	### Configuration — the `.env` file

file.content:
server does the parsing**, reusing the same adapters the UI relies on, so a client never has to
understand Antigravity's, Codex's, or Claude Code's file formats — and fixing an adapter fixes every
machine by upgrading the server alone.

Nothing duplicates. A trajectory is keyed by `(source, id)`, where the id comes from the transcript
itself (a Claude Code session UUID, a Codex rollout id, an Antigravity session directory) rather than
the path it was found at. Pushing the same session twice, or from two machines, updates one row —
and re-pushing unchanged content is a no-op the server reports as `skipped`.

These are the only endpoints that write. Set `INGEST_TOKEN` once the server is reachable from
another machine, and clients must then send `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

#### Securing an exposed deployment

Run on localhost only and the defaults are fine. The moment the server is reachable from another
machine — including any `docker compose --profile full up`, which publishes port 8080 on all
interfaces — treat it as exposed and do the following:

- **Set `INGEST_TOKEN`.** Without it, the write endpoints are open to anyone who can reach the port,
  and a large pushed body can pin significant heap (the request is buffered whole). The server logs a
  warning at startup whenever ingest is unauthenticated, so watch the boot log. Give the same token to
  `agent-ingest` via `AGENT_INGEST_TOKEN`.
- **Fail closed if you never want the open default.** Set `INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true` and the server
  refuses all ingest until a token is configured, so a missing `INGEST_TOKEN` can't silently leave the
  writes exposed.
- **Terminate TLS at a reverse proxy.** The app speaks plain HTTP; put nginx, Caddy, or a cloud load
  balancer in front to serve HTTPS, so the bearer token and transcripts aren't sent in the clear. If
  the proxy runs on the same host, keep `INGEST_TOKEN` set — an unauthenticated request forwarded from
  a local proxy is indistinguishable from a genuine localhost client.
- **Don't publish Postgres.** Keep the database on a private network; only the app needs to reach it.
  The default compose file exposes 5432 for local development — remove that port mapping in a shared
  deployment.

Read endpoints stay open (this is a single-user tool); put the whole app behind the proxy's auth if
you need to restrict who can view trajectories.

> [!NOTE]
> The eval run history previously lived in `~/.agybrainviz/eval-runs.jsonl`. That file is no longer
> read, so a history saved before this change won't appear. Nothing deletes it — you can remove it
> by hand once you're happy.

#### Remote / production deployment

To run the stack on a **server** that other machines reach over the network, layer the checked-in
production override (`docker-compose.prod.yml`) on the base compose. It closes the local-dev
conveniences: Postgres is no longer published to the network, and the app binds to localhost so a
reverse proxy is the only public entrypoint.

1. **Put your secrets in a `.env`** next to the compose files (compose reads it automatically), then
   `chmod 600 .env`:

   ```dotenv
   INGEST_TOKEN=<openssl rand -hex 32>
   INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true         # refuse ingest until the token is set (fail closed)
   GEMINI_API_KEY=<optional, for AI summaries>
   ```

2. **Bring up the stack**, stamping the image with a version so you can tell builds apart:

   ```bash
   APP_VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always) \
     docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml --profile full up -d --build
   curl -s localhost:8080/health        # {"status":"UP","version":"<your version>"}
   ```

   `GET /health` reports the running build's version, so you can confirm what's deployed at a glance
   (the CLI reports its own with `agent-ingest --version`).

3. **Terminate TLS at a reverse proxy** — Caddy gets you HTTPS with almost no config:

   ```
   # Caddyfile
   viz.example.com {
       reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:8080
   }
   ```

   See **Securing an exposed deployment** above for the full checklist (token, fail-closed, don't
   publish Postgres — the override already handles that last one).

4. **Push from each machine that has agent transcripts** — the transcripts live on your dev machines,
   not the server:

   ```bash
   export AGENT_INGEST_TOKEN=<the same token>
   agent-ingest --server https://viz.example.com
   ```

   Install `agent-ingest` from a [release](cli/README.md#install), or build it from source.

The `pgdata` volume keeps the store across restarts and rebuilds. To update: `git pull` and re-run the
same `up -d --build` command; the data is untouched.

### Configuration — the `.env` file

- **Set `API_TOKEN`.** It guards everything that isn't ingest: the stored transcripts, and the

  separate secret from `INGEST_TOKEN` on purpose — the ingest token is handed to every machine that
  pushes, the API token only to the people allowed to read.
- **Fail closed if you never want the open default.** Set `INGEST_REQUIRE_AUTH=true` (and
  `API_REQUIRE_AUTH=true`) and the server refuses those requests until a token is configured, so a
  missing token can't silently leave the endpoints exposed.

414	## Development & Tests
415	
416	Three suites cover the app; CI runs all of them on every pull request:

419	mise exec -- ./gradlew build   # backend JUnit + Spotless (format check)

421	npm test                       # frontend unit tests (Vitest)

426	**The tests need Docker running.** Nothing is faked: the store tests exercise a real Postgres rather
427	than an in-memory stand-in, because H2's Postgres mode supports neither `jsonb` nor the conditional
428	`ON CONFLICT … WHERE` the store relies on — an in-memory database would test something the app never
429	talks to.
430	
431	* `./gradlew build` starts its **own** Postgres via
432	  [Testcontainers](https://testcontainers.com/) and applies the real `db/schema.sql` to it. It does
433	  not use, or care about, your `docker compose` container — a suite that passed or failed depending
434	  on what happened to be listening on port 5432 would be worse than no suite at all.
435	* `npm run e2e` boots the packaged jar, which talks to a real database, so that one **does** need
436	  `docker compose up -d` first. Run it under `mise exec` so the jar gets Java 25.

file.content:
```

*(Note: Start-up times will be practically instantaneous compared to the standard JVM version).*

## Development & Tests

Three suites cover the app; CI runs all of them on every pull request:

```bash
mise exec -- ./gradlew build   # backend JUnit + Spotless (format check)
npm install                    # once, for the frontend/e2e tests
npm test                       # frontend unit tests (Vitest)
docker compose up -d           # the store the e2e jar talks to
mise exec -- npm run e2e       # end-to-end (Playwright) against a booted jar with seeded fixtures
```

**The tests need Docker running.** Nothing is faked: the store tests exercise a real Postgres rather
than an in-memory stand-in, because H2's Postgres mode supports neither `jsonb` nor the conditional
`ON CONFLICT … WHERE` the store relies on — an in-memory database would test something the app never
talks to.

* `./gradlew build` starts its **own** Postgres via
  [Testcontainers](https://testcontainers.com/) and applies the real `db/schema.sql` to it. It does
  not use, or care about, your `docker compose` container — a suite that passed or failed depending
  on what happened to be listening on port 5432 would be worse than no suite at all.
* `npm run e2e` boots the packaged jar, which talks to a real database, so that one **does** need
  `docker compose up -d` first. Run it under `mise exec` so the jar gets Java 25.

Code is auto-formatted by Spotless (prettier-java + prettier for JS); run `mise exec -- ./gradlew
spotlessApply` before committing.

cd cli && go test ./...        # the agent-ingest CLI (Go)
```

You are improving the vanilla-JS frontend of the repo at /home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer. Frontend lives in src/main/resources/public/ (app.js, index.html, modules/*.js), unit tests in src/test/js/*.test.js (vitest, jsdom; setup stubs in src/test/js/setup.js). Do not commit. IMPORTANT context: utils.js recently gained `apiFetch` (auth-aware fetch) and `renderMarkdown` (sanitized markdown) — all modules already use apiFetch instead of fetch. Do not change those semantics.

Apply these verified review findings:

1. Deduplicate copy-pasted code across modules:
   - `FLAVOR_LABELS` is duplicated in modules/insights.js, mine.js, eval.js, optimize.js — move one copy to modules/utils.js and import it.
   - `section(...)` and `round1(...)` helper functions are re-defined in several of those modules — if their implementations are identical, move to utils.js and import; if they differ, leave them and note it.
   - The repeated "container.innerHTML = loading… / try apiFetch / catch render red error" boilerplate in insights.js, mine.js, eval.js, optimize.js: extract only if it can be done without contorting the modules — a small shared helper like `fetchJson(url)` (throws on !ok, returns parsed JSON) is the right size. Don't over-abstract.

2. Keyboard accessibility (mouse-only today):
   - Conversation list items (`.conv-item`, built in app.js renderConversationsList) get `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and Enter/Space activation (the list uses event delegation for click — extend it with a keydown handler).
   - Stat cards in modules/stats.js that have click handlers (e.g. errors-stat-card) and drill rows in modules/drilldown.js: same treatment where they are interactive.

3. File-preview modal focus management (index.html #file-modal, modules/ui.js): when the modal opens, move focus to the close button; trap Tab inside the modal; on close, restore focus to the previously focused element. Escape-to-close already exists.

4. Global state cleanup: modules/stats.js writes `window.timelineStart` / `window.timelineTotalMs`, read in timeline.js and ui.js. Move these onto the shared `state` object exported from utils.js (keep `window.scrollToTime` as-is — it's used by inline onclick handlers). Update all readers.

Constraints:
- Zero-build vanilla JS ES modules; match existing code style and comment tone.
- Keep all 117 existing vitest tests passing; add/adjust tests where behavior is now testable (e.g. keyboard activation selects a conversation). Run `npm test` from the repo root.
- After edits run `./gradlew spotlessApply -q` from the repo root (formats the public JS; note vendor/ is excluded).
- Do NOT touch src/main/resources/public/vendor/, cli/, src/main/java/, .github/, Dockerfile, docker-compose*.

Return: summary of changes per file, test results tail, anything deliberately not done.

input.prompt:
You are improving the Go ingest CLI at /home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli (main.go, internal/scan/, internal/client/). Work ONLY inside cli/. Do not commit. Context: the CLI recently gained retry-with-backoff, signal handling, deterministic batch ordering, and duplicate-stem warnings — don't disturb those.

Task: eliminate re-hashing the whole corpus on every run. Today internal/scan reads every transcript file fully (os.ReadFile) and SHA-256s it on every invocation, even though nothing changed since the last run. Add a local incremental cache:

- Persist a small JSON cache file mapping session identity → {mtime, size, hash} (and whatever else is needed, e.g. the path). Sensible location: os.UserCacheDir()/agent-ingest/scan-cache.json (fall back gracefully — a missing/corrupt/unwritable cache must never fail the run, just degrade to full hashing).
- On scan, if a file's mtime and size match the cache entry, reuse the cached hash instead of reading + hashing the file. Note the scanner also does UTF-8 sanitization (ToValidUTF8) before hashing — make sure the cached hash corresponds to the same bytes that would be uploaded, and that the upload path still gets the file content when the server needs it (check how the scan result is used: if the raw content is needed for upload of changed sessions only, structure it so unchanged sessions skip the read entirely — they're filtered out by the server manifest diff anyway; changed sessions still read the file).
- Update the cache after a successful run (or as entries are computed). Add a `--no-cache` flag to force full re-hash, documented in the usage text.
- Keep behavior identical from the server's perspective.

Add tests: cache hit skips re-read (e.g. via a counter or by making the file unreadable after priming the cache — pick something non-flaky), mtime/size change invalidates the entry, corrupt cache file degrades gracefully, --no-cache bypasses.

When done run: `cd cli && gofmt -l . && go vet ./... && go test ./...` and fix anything failing. Also update cli/README.md if it documents flags.

Return: summary of changes (file:line), test output tail, anything deliberately not done.

input.prompt:
You are improving CI/build/deploy for the repo at /home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer. Do not commit. Do NOT touch src/main/resources/public/, src/main/java/, or cli/*.go — other work is in flight there. Context: docker-compose.yml now parameterizes POSTGRES_USER/PASSWORD with agentviz defaults, docker-compose.prod.yml requires POSTGRES_PASSWORD via `:?`, and the app has new API_TOKEN/API_REQUIRE_AUTH env vars — keep all that intact.

Apply these verified review findings:

1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: the Dockerfile is never built in CI. Add a job that builds the image and verifies it starts: e.g. `docker compose --profile full up -d --build --wait` (compose has healthchecks) then curl `http://localhost:8080/health` expecting 200, with `docker compose logs app` dumped on failure and cleanup. Keep it consistent with the existing job style/runner.
2. `ci.yml` hardcodes the Go version ("1.23") while `release-cli.yml` uses `go-version-file: cli/go.mod` — switch ci.yml to `go-version-file: cli/go.mod` so they can't desync.
3. `Dockerfile`: add a `HEALTHCHECK` instruction mirroring the compose /dev/tcp probe (the runtime image has bash but no curl — verify what the base image provides and use what works; the compose file's test command is the reference).
4. `mise.toml`: pins only java. Add node and go pins matching what CI uses (check ci.yml for the exact versions; go should match cli/go.mod).
5. Add `.github/dependabot.yml` covering npm (root), gradle, gomod (/cli), and github-actions, weekly.
6. `e2e/eval.spec.js` saves eval runs into the shared Postgres store and later assertions read them; with retries a re-run against a non-reset DB risks duplicate rows. Read the spec and make the saved-run assertions robust to pre-existing rows (scope assertions to run-specific identifiers or clean up in afterEach) — only if you can do it without weakening what's tested; otherwise report why not.
7. Sanity-check that README.md's Development & Tests section matches reality after your changes (it was just updated to say "Four suites … plus a native-image smoke test").

Validate what you can locally: `docker compose config -q`, YAML well-formedness of workflows (e.g. python yaml.safe_load), and if Docker is available and time permits, actually build the image (`docker build .`) — if that's too slow or unavailable, say so. Run `npx playwright test e2e/eval.spec.js` ONLY if a store and built jar are trivially available; otherwise skip and say so.

Return: summary of changes per file, validation results, anything deliberately not done.

input.prompt:
You are refactoring the Micronaut backend of the repo at /home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer. Do not commit. Work only under src/main/java/io/github/glaforge/agybrainviz/ and src/test/java/. Do NOT touch cli/, src/main/resources/public/, .github/, Dockerfile, docker-compose*.

Task: `AnalysisController.java` (~430 lines) is a god class — the HTTP controller embeds chunking, parallel LLM dispatch with a semaphore, progress simulation (the fakeProgress sleeper), recursive consolidation, and local-merge fallback. IMPORTANT recent changes to preserve exactly: summarize is now @Post; progress + running-task maps are keyed by taskKey(flavor, id); logging uses SLF4J (LOG); a shared static ObjectMapper MAPPER.

Refactor:
1. Extract the orchestration into a new `@Singleton AnalysisOrchestrator` service: chunking, the parallel analyze dispatch, consolidation (recursive + local-merge fallback), progress reporting, and caching interaction. The controller keeps: HTTP concerns, the aiConfig.isConfigured() guard, the cache read/delete decision or delegates it — choose the cleanest seam, but the controller should shrink to something like 100 lines and contain no chunking/consolidation logic.
2. Progress state: move progressMap/runningTasks into the orchestrator (still keyed by flavor+id); the controller's GET /progress endpoint reads through it. Prefer instance fields over statics now that it's a singleton.
3. While you're in there, replace the `fakeProgress` busy-sleeper (submits a Thread.sleep(2000) loop onto the IO executor for up to ~18s just to animate the progress bar) with a `java.util.concurrent.ScheduledExecutorService` (a small single-thread scheduled executor owned by the orchestrator, shut down via @PreDestroy) so no IO-pool thread is parked.
4. Behavior must be byte-for-byte identical from the HTTP client's perspective: same JSON responses, same "already running" guard, same cache/no-cache-on-fallback semantics, same progress percentages.

The integration tests in src/test/java/.../AnalysisControllerTest.java are the safety net — they must pass UNCHANGED (they use @MockBean for AnalyzerService/AiConfig/TokenCounter and a real Postgres via Testcontainers; Docker is available). Run: `./gradlew test --tests '*AnalysisControllerTest*'` and then the full `./gradlew test`. Also run `./gradlew spotlessApply -q` before the final test run (the build enforces formatting: prettier-java, 4-space indent, licenseHeaderFile spotless.license — new files need the license header).

If moving everything at once gets risky, extract in the order: consolidation+localMerge first, then chunk dispatch, then progress. Return: summary of the new structure, test results tail, anything deliberately not done.

The integration tests in src/test/java/.../AnalysisControllerTest.java are the safety net — they must pass UNCHANGED (they use @MockBean for AnalyzerService/AiConfig/TokenCounter and a real Postgres via Testcontainers; Docker is available). If moving everything at once gets risky, extract in the order: consolidation+localMerge first, then chunk dispatch, then progress. Return: summary of the new structure, test results tail, anything deliberately not done.

224	export function seedFixtures(home) {

226	  fs.rmSync(home, { recursive: true, force: true });
227	  fs.mkdirSync(gemini, { recursive: true });
228	
229	  const configPath = path.join(gemini, "config.txt");
230	  fs.writeFileSync(configPath, "parser.mode = strict\nparser.maxDepth = 32\n");
231	
232	  return { home, configPath, sessions: buildSessions(configPath) };

1	import { defineConfig, devices } from "@playwright/test";
2	import path from "node:path";
3	import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
4	
5	const projectRoot = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
6	const PORT = 8099;
7	const E2E_HOME = path.join(projectRoot, "build", "e2e-home");
8	
9	// Launch the prebuilt fat jar with user.home pointed at the seeded fixtures. A dummy GEMINI_API_KEY
10	// lets the cached-analysis path run without contacting the real LLM (the cache lookup is gated
11	// behind the key check). `find` (not a top-level glob) locates the jar even when the project
12	// version embeds a slash — e.g. a PR's GITHUB_REF_NAME of "1/merge" nests the jar in a subdir.
13	// `-Ddotenv.enabled=false` keeps the run hermetic: the jar boots in the project root, so a
14	// developer's local .env would otherwise be picked up and change the app's AI configuration.
15	const serverCommand =
16	  `sh -c 'java -Duser.home="${E2E_HOME}" -Dmicronaut.server.port=${PORT} ` +
17	  `-Ddotenv.enabled=false ` +
18	  `-jar "$(find build/libs -name "*-all.jar" | head -1)"'`;
19	
20	export default defineConfig({
21	  testDir: "./e2e",
22	  globalSetup: "./e2e/global-setup.mjs",
23	  timeout: 30_000,
24	  expect: { timeout: 7_000 },
25	  fullyParallel: false,
26	  workers: 1,
27	  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,
28	  retries: process.env.CI ? 1 : 0,
29	  reporter: process.env.CI
30	    ? [["github"], ["html", { open: "never" }]]
31	    : "list",
32	  use: {
33	    baseURL: `http://localhost:${PORT}`,
34	    trace: "on-first-retry",
35	  },
36	  projects: [
37	    // Pushes the fixture sessions to the store once the server is up; every test depends on it.
38	    { name: "setup", testMatch: /.*\.setup\.mjs/ },
39	    {
40	      name: "chromium",
41	      use: { ...devices["Desktop Chrome"] },
42	      dependencies: ["setup"],
43	    },
44	  ],
45	  webServer: {
46	    command: serverCommand,
47	    url: `http://localhost:${PORT}/api/brain/conversations`,
48	    // Locally reuse a running server for speed. Note: globalSetup re-seeds the fixture files (the
49	    // controllers read them per-request) but does NOT restart the server, so if you change the jar
50	    // or the -Duser.home target, kill the server on :8099 first. CI always starts fresh.
51	    reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
52	    timeout: 90_000,
53	    env: { GEMINI_API_KEY: "dummy-e2e-key", TZ: "UTC" },
54	  },
55	});

content.text:
The XSS regression spec is in place (Playwright auto-discovers new spec files, and the e2e server runs without tokens so the new auth filter stays open there). 

Status: the security pass, CLI reliability fixes, UI race/polling bugs, and the progressive transcript rendering are all done and tested. Four agents are now working in parallel on the remaining items: frontend dedup + keyboard accessibility, the CLI incremental scan cache, CI/Docker/ops hardening, and extracting the analysis orchestration out of the controller. I'll verify everything together (including the full e2e suite) once they report back.

## New structure

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/src/main/java/io/github/glaforge/agybrainviz/AnalysisController.java`** (86 lines, was ~430) — pure HTTP surface:
- `GET /conversations/{id}/progress` reads through `orchestrator.progress(flavor, id)` and maps to the same `ProgressResponse` DTO.
- `POST /conversations/{id}/summarize` keeps only the `aiConfig.isConfigured()` guard (serialized via the static `MAPPER`, preserved), then delegates to `orchestrator.summarize(flavorName, id, force)`. No chunking/consolidation logic remains.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/src/main/java/io/github/glaforge/agybrainviz/AnalysisOrchestrator.java`** (new, `@Singleton`, license header included) — owns everything else:
- `summarize(...)`: the "already running" guard (`runningTasks.putIfAbsent` on `taskKey(flavor, id)`), `sessions.exists` check, cache read / force-delete decision (I moved the cache seam into the orchestrator so the whole `runningTasks`-guarded critical section stays in one place — cache reads inside the guard is the original ordering), error-envelope catch, and `progressMap` cleanup.
- `analyzeAndConsolidate(...)`: chunking via `TranscriptParser.splitIntoSafeChunks`, then `analyzeChunksInParallel(...)` (IO-executor fan-out, `Semaphore(20)`, monotonic progress via `compute`), then recursive consolidation with the local-merge fallback and the no-cache-on-fallback + best-effort `summaries.upsert` semantics, all copied verbatim.
- `recursivelyConsolidate`, `localMerge`, `cap` moved unchanged.
- Progress state: `progressMap`/`runningTasks` are now **instance** `ConcurrentHashMap`s (were statics), still keyed by `taskKey(flavor, id)`. SLF4J `LOG` and static `MAPPER` preserved.
- `fakeProgress` sleeper replaced: a single-thread daemon `ScheduledExecutorService` owned by the orchestrator (`startConsolidationTicker`) ticks 90→99 at the same 2s cadence, cancelled in the same `finally`, and shut down via `@PreDestroy`. No IO-pool thread is parked anymore; the extra `AtomicBoolean consolidationDone` flag became unnecessary (cancellation covers it).

All client-visible strings, JSON shapes, progress percentages, and phase messages are byte-identical to the original.

## Test results

`AnalysisControllerTest` untouched and green: 14 tests, 0 failures. Full suite + `spotlessJavaCheck`:

```
> Task :test
BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 35s
total tests: 264, skipped: 0, failures: 0, errors: 0
```

## Deliberately not done / caveats

- The working tree contains many uncommitted modifications from a **concurrent workstream** (cli/, public/*.js, README, workflows, ApiAuthFilter, etc.) that predate/overlap my session — I did not touch any of them; my diff is only the two files above.
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` (full) currently fails on `spotlessJavascriptCheck` for `src/main/resources/public/modules/eval.js` and `stats.js` — those files are out of my allowed scope and were being actively edited by the other workstream while I ran (mtimes updated mid-check), so I did not reformat them. `spotlessJavaCheck` passes.
- I did not split the extraction into the suggested three phases since the single-pass move stayed green on the first test run.</result>

<result>All done. Tests pass (13 files, 128 tests) and spotless has formatted everything.

## Summary of changes

**src/main/resources/public/modules/utils.js**
- `state` gained `timelineStart` / `timelineTotalMs` (replacing the `window.*` globals).
- New shared exports: `FLAVOR_LABELS` (single copy of the source-label map), `section(title, color, bodyHtml)`, `round1(n)`, `fetchJson(url, options)` (apiFetch + throws `HTTP <status>` on `!res.ok` + parses JSON), and `wireButton(el, handler)` (click + Enter/Space activation for div-buttons, with Space `preventDefault`).
- `apiFetch` / `renderMarkdown` semantics untouched.

**modules/insights.js, mine.js, eval.js, optimize.js**
- Removed their duplicated `FLAVOR_LABELS`, `section`, and `round1` (eval + optimize) definitions; now imported from utils.js. All four copies were byte-identical, so nothing was left behind.
- Load paths now use `fetchJson`: `showInsights`, `showMining`, `showEval` (incl. `fetchHistory`), `runComparison`, `showOptimize`. eval.js keeps `apiFetch` for its POST/DELETE run persistence (no JSON body expected). Side effect: `showInsights` now also treats a non-2xx as a failure (it previously tried to parse it).

**modules/drilldown.js**
- `toggleDrill` uses `fetchJson`; `.drill-bar` and `.drill-session` rows get `role="button" tabindex="0"` and are wired via `wireButton`, so drill rows expand and sessions open from the keyboard.

**modules/stats.js**
- Writes `state.timelineStart` / `state.timelineTotalMs` instead of `window.*`.
- The five interactive stat cards get `role="button" tabindex="0"` (the OUTCOME card only when there are errors, matching its click wiring) and use `wireButton` for Enter/Space parity.

**modules/timeline.js, modules/ui.js**
- Read timeline bounds from `state` instead of `window`. `window.scrollToTime` left as-is (inline onclick handlers).

**modules/ui.js + index.html (modal)**
- Opening the file preview records `document.activeElement`, shows the modal, and focuses `#close-modal-btn`; `closeFileModal` restores focus to the remembered element. A `trapModalTab` handler keeps Tab/Shift+Tab cycling inside the open modal (shares the existing Escape keydown listener). index.html: `#file-modal` gains `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="file-modal-title"`, close button gains `aria-label`.

**app.js**
- `.conv-item` rows get `role="button"` and `tabIndex = 0`; the list's event delegation gains a keydown branch so Enter/Space selects the focused session.

**Tests** (117 → 128, all passing)
- `utils.test.js`: fetchJson success/failure, round1, section escaping, FLAVOR_LABELS, wireButton activation keys.
- `app.test.js`: conv items are focusable buttons; Enter selects a session end-to-end.
- `stats.test.js`: cards keyboard-toggle their charts; inert OUTCOME card has no button role, errored one does.
- `drilldown.test.js`: Enter on a drill bar expands it.
- `ui.test.js`: focus moves to the close button on open and is restored on close.
- `insights.test.js`: one existing mock updated to include `ok: true` (required now that the load path checks `res.ok` — the old mock didn't model a real Response).

Test tail:
```
 Test Files  13 passed (13)
      Tests  128 passed (128)
```

**Deliberately not done**
- The "loading… / try / catch red error" shells were kept in each module (only the fetch-parse-throw core was extracted as `fetchJson`) — the loading texts and error messages differ per view, and unifying them would contort the modules.
- `window.scrollToTime` intentionally left on `window` (used by inline `onclick` in generated timeline HTML).
- No focus trap for `#conv-popover` (it's `pointer-events: none` hover chrome, not interactive). Nothing committed; `vendor/`, `cli/`, Java, CI files untouched by me (the working tree contains unrelated concurrent modifications I left alone).</result>

<result>All done. The working tree contains other in-flight changes (as expected); my edits touched only the six intended files and none of the restricted paths.

## Summary of changes

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/.github/workflows/ci.yml`**
- New `docker` job ("Docker image (compose smoke)", `ubuntu-latest`, 20-min timeout, matching the existing job style): `docker compose --profile full up -d --build --wait` (compose healthchecks gate `--wait`), then curls `http://localhost:8080/health` asserting HTTP 200, with `docker compose --profile full logs app` on failure and `down -v` teardown on `always()`.
- `cli` job: `go-version: "1.23"` replaced with `go-version-file: cli/go.mod` (with a comment mirroring release-cli.yml), so the two workflows can't desync.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/Dockerfile`**
- Added a `HEALTHCHECK --interval=10s --timeout=5s --retries=12 --start-period=40s` using bash's `/dev/tcp` probe of `/health`, exec-form, mirroring the compose reference (which still takes precedence under compose). I verified the `eclipse-temurin:25-jre` base directly: it has bash but no curl and no wget, so `/dev/tcp` is the right tool.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/mise.toml`**
- Added `node = "22"` (matches CI's setup-node) and `go = "1.23"` (matches `cli/go.mod`, which CI now tracks too), with comments.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/.github/dependabot.yml`** (new)
- Weekly updates for npm (`/`), gradle (`/`), gomod (`/cli`), and github-actions (`/`).

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/e2e/eval.spec.js`**
- Saved-run assertions are now scoped to run-specific identifiers without weakening coverage: a `saveRun()` helper snapshots the `data-saved-at` set, clicks save, waits for row count +1, and returns the one new `savedAt`. The "records it in history" test asserts on that specific row; the A/B test ticks exactly the two rows it saved (via `div:has(> button.run-del-btn[data-saved-at=...])`) instead of `nth(0)/nth(1)`; the delete test targets its own run's delete button. Added a `test.afterEach` that deletes every run a test saved via `DELETE /api/eval/runs?savedAt=...` (safe: serial workers, and the endpoint takes only `savedAt` — confirmed in `EvalController.java`), so retries against a non-reset store start clean and history doesn't grow unboundedly. Tests 1–2 (no writes) unchanged.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/README.md`**
- Development & Tests now says CI runs the four suites "plus a native-image smoke test and a Docker image build-and-boot smoke"; the mise blurb now says it pins the toolchain (Java plus Node/Go), matching the new mise.toml.

## Validation results

- `docker compose config -q`: passes.
- YAML well-formedness (ruby YAML — no python3 `yaml` module on this machine): ci.yml, release-cli.yml, dependabot.yml, docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.prod.yml all parse. actionlint not installed.
- `node --check e2e/eval.spec.js`: OK; `npx playwright test --list` collects all 5 eval tests plus setup.
- `docker build .`: ran to completion successfully (image built). I then booted the image standalone and watched the baked-in HEALTHCHECK go from a "connection refused" probe during startup to status `healthy` — the probe works exactly as written. Test image and container cleaned up afterward.

## Deliberately not done

- Did not run `npx playwright test e2e/eval.spec.js`: port 5432 on this machine is held by an unrelated project's Postgres (`travel-app-db-1`), so the suite would connect to the wrong database (or fail auth) and starting this repo's compose Postgres would conflict on the port. The spec was validated by syntax check, Playwright collection, and cross-checking every selector/endpoint against `src/main/resources/public/modules/eval.js` and `EvalController.java`.
- Did not run the full compose smoke locally for the same port-conflict reason; CI runners start clean so the new job is unaffected.
- No commits made, and no edits under `src/main/resources/public/`, `src/main/java/`, or `cli/`.</result>

```
ok  	github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/cli	5.344s
ok  	github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/client	(cached)
ok  	github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/scan	0.014s
```
(`gofmt -l .` clean, `go vet ./...` clean; the new tests also pass under `-race`.)

## Summary of changes

**New: `/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/scan/cache.go`**
- `Cache` type keyed by session identity (`source + "\x00" + id`) with entries `{path, mtimeNano, size, hash}`; the hash is of the UTF-8-sanitized content (the exact bytes uploaded), never the raw file bytes (cache.go:41-47).
- `DefaultCachePath()` → `os.UserCacheDir()/agent-ingest/scan-cache.json`, `""` when unavailable (cache.go:31).
- `LoadCache` never fails: missing/unreadable/corrupt/version-mismatched files yield an empty cache (cache.go:69). `Save` writes atomically (temp + rename) and returns errors only for an optional warning (cache.go:117). `lookup`/`store` are nil-receiver-safe, so a nil `*Cache` disables caching entirely.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/scan/scan.go`**
- `Session` gains `Path` and an unexported `rawLoaded`; new `(*Session).LoadRaw()` reads + sanitizes on demand (scan.go:73-89).
- `Scan` keeps its old signature (delegates with nil cache); new `ScanWithCache` threads the cache through `scanFlat`/`scanAntigravity` (scan.go:105-115).
- `readSession` (scan.go:243): on a path+size+mtime cache hit it skips the read entirely and returns a Session with only the cached hash; on a miss it hashes and stores under the pre-read stat, so a stat/read race self-invalidates on the next run.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/main.go`**
- New `--no-cache` flag (main.go:97) and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` env override for the cache path (main.go:145); both documented in the usage text (main.go:432, 445).
- `run` loads the cache (unless `--no-cache`), scans via `ScanWithCache`, and saves right after the scan — best-effort, warning-only on failure (main.go:167-184).
- `changedSessions` (main.go:290) now calls `LoadRaw` only for sessions the server manifest says changed, so unchanged sessions never get read — only stat'ed. A file that vanished between scan and push is skipped with a warning, matching the scanner's tolerance.

**Tests** — `internal/scan/cache_test.go` (new): cache hit proven via a non-flaky stat-preserving rewrite (same-length content + `os.Chtimes` restore — only a real re-read could see the new bytes), mtime-change and size-change invalidation, corrupt file and unknown-version degradation, unwritable path never failing the scan, nil-cache full hashing, and `LoadRaw` on-demand read. `main_test.go`: `runCLI` now injects a throwaway `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` so tests never touch the real user cache dir (main_test.go:148); end-to-end `TestScanCacheSkipsRehashingUnchangedFilesAcrossRuns` and `TestNoCacheFlagForcesAFullRehash` (main_test.go:704, 733); help test asserts `--no-cache` and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE`.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/README.md`** — new paragraph on the scan cache, `--no-cache` flag row, and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` env row.

## Server-perspective behavior
Unchanged: hashes are identical (same sanitized bytes), the manifest diff is always consulted, pushed payloads carry the same fields, and retry/backoff, signal handling, batch ordering, and duplicate-stem warnings are untouched.

## Deliberately not done
- No pruning of cache entries for deleted files (stale entries are harmless and keep the file small; they self-invalidate on any change).
- `LoadRaw` does not re-verify the cached hash against re-read content mid-run; if a file mutates without changing size+mtime after a manifest mismatch, the pushed content is authoritative and the next run self-heals via the server manifest.
- Antigravity `summary.json` files are still read every run — they are small, and their sync path diffs against the summary manifest, not the local cache.
- No cache-file locking for concurrent CLI invocations; the atomic rename means the worst case is one run's entries winning, never corruption.</result>

(`gofmt -l .` clean, `go vet ./...` clean; the new tests also pass under `-race`.)

## Summary of changes

**New: `/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/scan/cache.go`**
- `Cache` type keyed by session identity (`source + "\x00" + id`) with entries `{path, mtimeNano, size, hash}`; the hash is of the UTF-8-sanitized content (the exact bytes uploaded), never the raw file bytes (cache.go:41-47).
- `DefaultCachePath()` → `os.UserCacheDir()/agent-ingest/scan-cache.json`, `""` when unavailable (cache.go:31).
- `LoadCache` never fails: missing/unreadable/corrupt/version-mismatched files yield an empty cache (cache.go:69). `Save` writes atomically (temp + rename) and returns errors only for an optional warning (cache.go:117). `lookup`/`store` are nil-receiver-safe, so a nil `*Cache` disables caching entirely.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/internal/scan/scan.go`**
- `Session` gains `Path` and an unexported `rawLoaded`; new `(*Session).LoadRaw()` reads + sanitizes on demand (scan.go:73-89).
- `Scan` keeps its old signature (delegates with nil cache); new `ScanWithCache` threads the cache through `scanFlat`/`scanAntigravity` (scan.go:105-115).
- `readSession` (scan.go:243): on a path+size+mtime cache hit it skips the read entirely and returns a Session with only the cached hash; on a miss it hashes and stores under the pre-read stat, so a stat/read race self-invalidates on the next run.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/main.go`**
- New `--no-cache` flag (main.go:97) and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` env override for the cache path (main.go:145); both documented in the usage text (main.go:432, 445).
- `run` loads the cache (unless `--no-cache`), scans via `ScanWithCache`, and saves right after the scan — best-effort, warning-only on failure (main.go:167-184).
- `changedSessions` (main.go:290) now calls `LoadRaw` only for sessions the server manifest says changed, so unchanged sessions never get read — only stat'ed. A file that vanished between scan and push is skipped with a warning, matching the scanner's tolerance.

**Tests** — `internal/scan/cache_test.go` (new): cache hit proven via a non-flaky stat-preserving rewrite (same-length content + `os.Chtimes` restore — only a real re-read could see the new bytes), mtime-change and size-change invalidation, corrupt file and unknown-version degradation, unwritable path never failing the scan, nil-cache full hashing, and `LoadRaw` on-demand read. `main_test.go`: `runCLI` now injects a throwaway `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` so tests never touch the real user cache dir (main_test.go:148); end-to-end `TestScanCacheSkipsRehashingUnchangedFilesAcrossRuns` and `TestNoCacheFlagForcesAFullRehash` (main_test.go:704, 733); help test asserts `--no-cache` and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE`.

**`/home/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer/cli/README.md`** — new paragraph on the scan cache, `--no-cache` flag row, and `AGENT_INGEST_CACHE` env row.

## Server-perspective behavior
Unchanged: hashes are identical (same sanitized bytes), the manifest diff is always consulted, pushed payloads carry the same fields, and retry/backoff, signal handling, batch ordering, and duplicate-stem warnings are untouched.

## Deliberately not done
- No pruning of cache entries for deleted files (stale entries are harmless and keep the file small; they self-invalidate on any change).
- `LoadRaw` does not re-verify the cached hash against re-read content mid-run; if a file mutates without changing size+mtime after a manifest mismatch, the pushed content is authoritative and the next run self-heals via the server manifest.
- Antigravity `summary.json` files are still read every run — they are small, and their sync path diffs against the summary manifest, not the local cache.
- No cache-file locking for concurrent CLI invocations; the atomic rename means the worst case is one run's entries winning, never corruption.</result>

content.text:
Everything is done and verified. Here's the complete picture of what changed — all of it is in the working tree, uncommitted (59 files, +1626/−892).

## Security pass
- **Stored XSS closed**: all transcript markdown now goes through `renderMarkdown()` — `marked` output sanitized by DOMPurify (vendored at `public/vendor/purify.min.js` so the sanitizer can never be missing; `file://` links whitelisted for the preview modal). Raw interpolations escaped: tool names, step type/source badges, citation URLs (plus `rel="noopener"`). marked and highlight.js are now version-pinned with SRI hashes. A new e2e spec (`e2e/security.spec.js`) feeds a hostile transcript through the real app and asserts nothing executes.
- **API auth**: new `ApiAuthFilter` guards everything that isn't ingest (`/api/brain`, `/analysis`, `/eval`, `/insights`, `/mine`, `/optimize`) behind an optional `API_TOKEN` — deliberately a different secret from `INGEST_TOKEN`, with the same `API_REQUIRE_AUTH` fail-closed switch. The web UI prompts once on 401 and remembers the token. Both filters share one constant-time `BearerTokens` helper.
- **`summarize` is now POST** (it spends LLM tokens and invalidates cache), the file-preview endpoint resolves symlinks before the sandbox check (with a regression test), and `docker-compose.prod.yml` now refuses to start without a real `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`. README and `.env.example` document all of it.

## Correctness fixes
- **CLI**: the 1 MiB body cap now applies only to error responses (large manifests no longer silently truncate), transient failures retry with backoff, Ctrl-C cancels cleanly, duplicate filename stems warn instead of silently colliding, and batch order is deterministic.
- **UI**: a generation guard stops a slow transcript fetch from rendering into the wrong session; the analysis progress poller can no longer loop forever after a failed fetch; analysis progress is keyed by `(flavor, id)` end-to-end so same-id sessions in different sources don't share state.

## Performance
- Transcript bodies render progressively — headers paint instantly, markdown/JSON work fills in during idle time and immediately on click. The CLI keeps a local `mtime/size/hash` cache (`--no-cache` to bypass), so scheduled runs stat files instead of re-reading the whole corpus. Shared static `ObjectMapper`s replace per-request allocation.

## Structure & ops
- `AnalysisController` shrank from ~430 to 86 lines; a new `AnalysisOrchestrator` owns chunking, fan-out, consolidation, and progress (the fake-progress sleeper now uses a scheduled executor instead of parking an IO thread). SLF4J everywhere, no more `printStackTrace`.
- Frontend dedup (`FLAVOR_LABELS`, `section`, `round1`, `fetchJson` in one place), full keyboard accessibility (session list, stat cards, drill rows, modal focus trap + ARIA), timeline bounds moved off `window.*` globals.
- CI now builds and boot-tests the Docker image, the Go version comes from `go.mod` everywhere, the Dockerfile has a `HEALTHCHECK`, `mise.toml` pins node/go, Dependabot covers npm/gradle/gomod/actions, and the eval e2e spec is retry-safe.

**Verification**: Java 264 tests, frontend 128 tests, Go suite (including `-race` on the new code), and all 35 Playwright e2e tests pass; Spotless is clean; the Docker image was built and probed healthy. One interlock worth knowing: `e2e/vendor/marked.min.js` must stay byte-identical to the SRI-pinned version in `index.html` (I upgraded it to 15.0.12 and documented this in `test-base.js`).

**Deliberately left**: unit tests for the four LLM-backed services (still a gap), the Go module path inconsistency (renaming would churn the release workflows), and the ingest API's 200-with-`failed`-count semantics (the CLI already inspects the count). 

Want me to commit this — either as one commit or split into logical commits (security / CLI / UI fixes / refactor / ops) on a branch?

feat(security): guard the read/compute API and harden exposed deployments

The ingest endpoints were the only guarded routes, but everything else is
just as sensitive on a server reachable off localhost: the stored
transcripts, the endpoints that spend LLM tokens (summarize, mine,
optimize, eval's judge), and the eval-run writes/deletes.

- New ApiAuthFilter guards all non-ingest /api routes behind an optional
  API_TOKEN, with an API_REQUIRE_AUTH fail-closed switch mirroring
  ingest. Deliberately a separate secret from INGEST_TOKEN: the ingest
  token goes to every pushing machine, the API token only to readers.
- Both filters share one constant-time BearerTokens comparison.
- The file-preview endpoint resolves symlinks before re-checking the
  ~/.gemini sandbox, so a planted link can't read files outside it.
- docker-compose.prod.yml refuses to start until POSTGRES_PASSWORD is
  set; the base compose parameterizes the dev credentials and forwards
  API_TOKEN/API_REQUIRE_AUTH to the app container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1

fix(analysis): summarize via POST, per-source task keys, orchestrator extraction

- summarize is now a POST: it spends LLM tokens and (with force=true)
  deletes the cached summary, so it must not be reachable by
  safe/idempotent GETs (browser prefetchers, proxies, crawlers).
- The in-flight guard and progress map are keyed by (flavor, id) instead
  of id alone — two sources can hold a session with the same id, and
  they must not share a progress bar or an "already running" slot. The
  progress endpoint takes the flavor as a query param.
- The chunking / parallel-dispatch / consolidation pipeline moves out of
  the controller into a new AnalysisOrchestrator singleton; the
  controller shrinks to the HTTP surface. Client-visible behavior is
  unchanged (same JSON, same progress phases and percentages).
- The progress-bar ticker during consolidation uses a scheduled executor
  instead of parking an IO-pool thread in a sleep loop.
- SLF4J replaces System.out/printStackTrace; a shared static
  ObjectMapper replaces per-request allocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1

lastPrompt: Lets split into logical commits on a branch

input.command:
git add build.gradle src/main/resources/public src/test/js e2e/fixtures.mjs e2e/security.spec.js e2e/sessions.spec.js e2e/test-base.js e2e/vendor/marked.min.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(web): sanitize untrusted transcripts, fix races, render progressively

Security — transcript content is untrusted (it is whatever the agent,
the user, or a web page the agent read produced):
- All markdown now renders through renderMarkdown(): marked output
  sanitized by DOMPurify before touching innerHTML. DOMPurify is
  vendored (public/vendor/) so the sanitizer can never be missing;
  file:// links stay allowed for the inline preview modal.
- Raw interpolations escaped: tool names, step type/source badges, and
  web-search citation URLs (plus rel="noopener").
- marked and highlight.js are version-pinned with subresource-integrity
  hashes; the e2e vendored marked copy is upgraded to the exact pinned
  version (SRI silently blocks a mismatched file).
- New e2e spec drives a hostile fixture transcript (script tags, onerror
  handlers, attacker-controlled tool name) through the real app and
  asserts nothing executes.

Correctness:
- A generation guard in selectConversation stops a slow transcript fetch
  from rendering into a newer session's view.
- The analysis progress poller stops in a finally block, so a failed
  summarize fetch no longer polls /progress forever; polling passes the
  flavor and summarize is called as POST to match the API.
- All API calls go through apiFetch, which attaches the API_TOKEN bearer
  (prompting once on 401) now that the server can guard the API.

Performance: step bodies render progressively — headers paint instantly,
markdown/JSON work fills in during idle time and immediately on click.

Accessibility & structure: session list, stat cards, and drill rows are
keyboard-operable (role, tabindex, Enter/Space); the file modal traps
focus and restores it on close; FLAVOR_LABELS/section/round1/fetchJson
deduplicated into utils.js; timeline bounds move off window.* globals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1

input.command:
git add cli && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cli): retries, incremental scan cache, and safer failure behavior

- The 1 MiB response-body cap now applies only to error bodies; a
  successful manifest GET is read in full, so a store past ~9k sessions
  no longer gets its manifest silently truncated (which made the source
  permanently unsyncable).
- Transient failures (connection errors, 429, 5xx) retry with bounded
  exponential backoff; 4xx never retries; POST bodies are rewound via
  GetBody; backoff sleeps respect context cancellation.
- SIGINT/SIGTERM cancel in-flight requests cleanly via
  signal.NotifyContext.
- A local scan cache (os.UserCacheDir()/agent-ingest/scan-cache.json,
  override with AGENT_INGEST_CACHE, bypass with --no-cache) stores
  mtime/size/hash per session, so scheduled runs stat unchanged files
  instead of re-reading and re-hashing the whole corpus; transcripts are
  read lazily only for sessions the server manifest says changed. The
  hash still covers the exact UTF-8-sanitized bytes that get uploaded,
  and a missing/corrupt/unwritable cache degrades to full hashing.
- Two sessions sharing a filename stem in different project dirs no
  longer silently overwrite each other: first wins, with a stderr
  warning naming both paths.
- A bearer token sent to a non-loopback http:// server warns about
  cleartext; batch order is deterministic (sorted per source); a failed
  --json report write exits non-zero instead of being swallowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1
